data.title: Vistra Maintained at Buy as Analyst Trims Price Target to $209 While Highlighting AI-Driven Growth Opportunities
data.description:
Analyst Carly Davenport from Goldman Sachs maintained a Buy rating on Vistra Corp and decreased the price target to $209.00 from $212.00.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Carly Davenport has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Vistra’s strategic positioning and new growth avenues. The company’s role as a founding investor and preferred power provider in the newly formed Helix Digital Infrastructure platform gives it direct exposure to rising power needs from hyperscale AI data centers, supported by over $10 billion in committed capital.

Davenport views this structure as an attractive, incremental way for Vistra to capture hyperscaler demand beyond traditional bilateral power contracts. While the official price target was modestly reduced from $212 to $209, she still sees meaningful upside for the stock based on Vistra’s ability to monetize AI-driven load growth and leverage multiple channels for long-term value creation.

Davenport covers the Utilities sector, focusing on stocks such as Duke Energy, Eversource Energy, and Ameren. According to TipRanks, Davenport has an average return of 2.6% and a 53.66% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on June 4, Wells Fargo also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $259.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: MetaVia: Buy Rating Reiterated as Synergistic Cardiometabolic Pipeline Supports Unchanged $8 Price Target
data.description:
Jason McCarthy, an analyst from Maxim Group, maintained the Buy rating on MetaVia. The associated price target remains the same with $8.00.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Jason McCarthy has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, centered on MetaVia’s advancing cardiometabolic pipeline and newly reported synergistic combination data. He highlights that vanoglipel, an oral GPR119 agonist, showed compelling preclinical synergy when paired with resmetirom and metformin in MASH and obesity models, driving marked weight and fat mass reductions despite neither partner drug being a stand‑alone weight loss agent.

He also points to MetaVia’s broader platform, including DA-1726, a dual GLP‑1/glucagon agonist with ongoing Phase 1 work and an important titration readout expected in 4Q26, as a differentiated approach to increasing energy expenditure while managing appetite. In his view, the strength of the combination data, the focus on high‑value metabolic indications, and the optionality created by oral vanoglipel as a partner to other agents support the current valuation and justify maintaining an $8 price target and a Buy rating on MTVA shares.

According to TipRanks, McCarthy is an analyst with an average return of -14.4% and a 28.40% success rate. McCarthy covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Plus Therapeutics, Medicus Pharma Ltd, and Atossa Therapeutics.

In another report released on June 9, H.C. Wainwright also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $20.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating Reiterated on Oryzon as Advancing AML Franchise and Strong Iadademstat Data Support Unchanged $10 Price Target
data.description:
Maxim Group analyst Jason McCarthy maintained a Buy rating on Oryzon Genomics SA yesterday and set a price target of $10.00.

Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Jason McCarthy has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Oryzon’s advancing AML franchise and the strength of recent iadademstat data. The company’s Phase 1b ALICE-2 trial in first-line unfit AML showed exceptionally high response metrics with the venetoclax/azacitidine triplet, including broad activity across high‑risk genomic subgroups and meaningful transition rates to transplant, which together support a differentiated clinical profile.

These results, together with encouraging signals from the FRIDA study in relapsed/refractory FLT3‑mutant AML, underpin a clear path toward a pivotal ALICE-3 program and potential value inflection at the upcoming ALICE-2 top-line readout. Given this clinical momentum, mutation-agnostic mechanism, and the approaching catalyst at ASH, McCarthy maintains a Buy rating and keeps his $10 price target unchanged, reflecting confidence in longer-term upside despite inherent development risk.

According to TipRanks, McCarthy is an analyst with an average return of -14.4% and a 28.40% success rate. McCarthy covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Plus Therapeutics, Medicus Pharma Ltd, and Atossa Therapeutics.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
